name: MO medium + 15 mM sodium lactate / 10 mM sodium sulfite (respiratory) (PMC8794893)
description: MO medium + 15 mM sodium lactate / 10 mM sodium sulfite (respiratory) — medium reported in PMC8794893 (complex/undefined base — extracts not BiGG-mappable).

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC8794893 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Complex medium: chemically-defined portion mapped to BiGG; biological extracts listed in `unmapped`. Added via GrowthDB curation.
provenance.formulation_warning: workflow correction sparse; kept original — Organism = Desulfovibrio vulgaris Hildenborough, a strict anaerobic sulfate-reducer grown ANAEROBICALLY. Current file's O2 uptake component and 'facultative' oxygen are wrong and were removed. This is MO medium (respiratory) with 15 mM lactate donor + 10 mM sulfite acceptor. The MO mineral base is defined in cited ref 59 (Zane, Yen & Wall 2010, Appl Environ Microbiol 76:5500) and is NOT restated in this paper; base salt amounts below are from the standard MO defined medium (verify against ref 59) hence medium confidence. No yeast extract in the growth medium (MO, not MOY). Donor/acceptor concentrations are stated verbatim in the paper (high confidence).
provenance.verification_evidence: D. vulgaris H WT and the two modified strains were grown anaerobically at 37 °C in MO medium (59). Under respiratory conditions, 15 or 30 mM sodium lactate was added as the electron donor, and sodium sulfate (30 mM), sodium sulfite (10 or 20 mM) or sodium thiosulfate (10 or 20 mM) was added as the terminal electron acceptor.
